sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel movement system documented for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Horse Riding
special_mechanics.description: Player party can ride horses for faster overworld traversal

special_mechanics.name: Ship/Skiff Navigation
special_mechanics.description: Player can sail ships and row skiffs for water-based movement

special_mechanics.name: Magic Carpet
special_mechanics.description: Increases party speed and can float over most terrain except mountains. Can traverse swamps, rivers, coastal waters. Causes damage over deep sea or lava.

physics_values.terrain_traversal: swamp, river, coastal water safe; deep water and lava cause damage

special_mechanics.name: Grapple
special_mechanics.description: Used to scale small mountains; large mountains remain impassable

special_mechanics.name: Moongates
special_mechanics.description: Magical teleportation doorways appearing at night, relocatable via digging moonstones

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Qualitative gameplay data only. Ultima V is an RPG with party-based movement rather than precise platformer physics. No jump mechanics or gravity values documented for NES version. Movement primarily involves overworld traversal via walking, riding, and various magical transportation methods.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Ultima V: Warriors of Destiny
